steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The passage describes the method as “apparently crude,” then reveals it “yielded a relatively close approximation.” A primitive method unexpectedly gave accurate results — the transition must bridge these contradictory ideas.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Still is a contrast word meaning however or yet — it directly signals the shift from crude method to accurate results, which is exactly what the passage needs.

Similarly and In other words treat the ideas as equivalent, while Fittingly implies the result is expected rather than surprising.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
